data.title: Strong IPO pipeline making Indian markets more vulnerable to FII flows, says Axis MF
data.description:
representative image

The stock market is becoming more vulnerable to foreign investor behavior because the supply of new stocks in the market is accelerating, said Ashish Gupta, chief investment officer of Axis Mutual Fund in its Acumen report.

The report says that in Q2FY25, companies issued new shares to the tune of $21 billion, up from $15 billion in the previous quarter. This was more than the $18 billion invested by mutual funds during the quarter. However, since foreign investors added $11.6 billion, the market continued to rise.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Since FY24, the supply of new stocks has been 1.5 times the money flowing into mutual funds. But the market didn't feel the impact because foreign investors added $35 billion from April 2023 to September 2024, helping to absorb the extra supply.

In the second half of the year, the supply of new stocks in the market is expected to be much higher than in the first half, the report said. It is because 91 companies plan to raise $17 billion through Initial Public Offerings (IPOs). Another 70 listed companies have recently received board approval to raise $16 billion through Qualified Institutional Placements (QIPs). Further, secondary sales (when promoters and private equity investors sell existing shares) are expected to add another $22 billion, similar to the first half.

A large amount of money is being raised by companies through selling shares, which could help speed up private investment in the economy, the report said. Unlike previous cycles, this time, companies are relying more on equity (selling shares) rather than borrowing money (debt).
In the last 18 months, companies raised $39 billion through IPOs and QIPs, with $25 billion going directly to the companies for investment. In the second half of FY25, companies are expected to raise another $24 billion, which they can use for things like expanding their businesses, building new facilities (capex), or making acquisitions.

The report said that judicious use of the capital by corporates would be a key determinant for sustaining the earnings growth momentum as easy capital availability can tempt companies into low return capex, unrelated diversifications and expensive acquisitions.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: FIIs net buy shares worth Rs 3,665 crore, DIIs net sell Rs 251-crore shares
data.description:
At close today, the Sensex was up 597.67 points or 0.74 percent at 80,845.75, and the Nifty was up 181.10 points or 0.75 percent at 24,457.15.

Domestic institutional investors (DIIs) net sold shares worth Rs 251 crore, while on the other hand, foreign institutional investors (FIIs) net bought shares worth Rs 3,665 crore, provisional data from NSE showed.

During the trading session, DIIs bought Rs 14,553-crore shares and sold shares worth Rs 14,804 crore, and FIIs purchased Rs 22,450-crore shares while offloading equities worth Rs 18,785 crore.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

For the year so far, FIIs have net sold shares worth Rs 2.96 lakh crore, while DIIs have net bought equities worth Rs 5.73 lakh crore.

Market View

At close today, the Sensex was up 597.67 points or 0.74 percent at 80,845.75, and the Nifty was up 181.10 points or 0.75 percent at 24,457.15.

Top Nifty gainers included Adani Ports, NTPC, Adani Enterprises, Axis Bank and SBI, while losers were Bharti Airtel, ITC, Hero MotoCorp, HDFC Life, and Sun Pharma.

Among sectors, except FMCG, all other indices ended in the green.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Also read: Taking Stock: Nifty above 24,450, Sensex up 598 pts; media, PSU banks shine

On today's market, Vinod Nair, Head of Research, Geojit Financial Services noted that the benchmark indices continued to demonstrate resilience amidst positive global sentiment.

"Investors are now focusing on potential growth drivers, assuming that weak economic data has already been reflected in the recent lacklustre corporate earnings. The immediate attention is expected to remain on the RBI's interest rate guidance and liquidity management. Banking stocks experienced the highest gains due to their interest rate sensitivity, while metal stocks benefited from increased import duties and favourable manufacturing data from China," Nair said.

data.title: Bearish options bets on Rupee jump 200% on surprise GDP slowdown
data.description:
Indian rupee banknotes.

Options traders have increased bearish wagers on the Indian rupee after data showed a sharp slowdown in the nation’s economic growth, heaping further pressure on the currency that’s hit a series of record lows in recent days.

Data from the Depository Trust and Clearing Corp. show the volume of dollar-rupee calls trading climbed to $1.9 billion on Monday in the non-deliverable options market, up from about $600 million Friday.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

“This represents a significant jump in rupee bearish bets, particularly given that the bulk of the strikes were above the current spot,” said Akshay Chinchalkar, head of research at Axis Securities Ltd.


The rupee, which hit a new low of 84.7625 per dollar Tuesday, is likely to weaken toward 86 by the final quarter of 2025, as less favourable economic growth crimps foreign inflows, according to Michael Wan, senior currency analyst at MUFG Bank.

The spike in bearish bets has been accompanied by an increase in the rupee’s one-month implied volatility versus the dollar. This measure rose to 3.27% on Tuesday, the highest since Aug. 6. It was at 2.55% on Friday.

“A combination of a weaker Indian data and broad-based USD strength seems to have created renewed investor interest toward INR via option strategies, thus creating demand for implied volatility,” said Nathan Swami, head of FX trading for Asia Pacific at Citigroup Inc.

Even small changes in intraday volatility, risk sentiment, or options activity can lead to an outsized surge in implied volatility, which is “precisely what’s happening now,” he said.

The volatility has upended the stability that has been the defining feature of the local currency market in recent months. The rupee has been one of the least volatile currencies among the emerging markets in 2024, mainly due to the central bank’s intervention strategy.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The fresh phase of rupee weakness comes ahead of the Reserve Bank of India’s policy meeting on Friday, with the sharp growth slowdown stoking speculation of the authority announcing liquidity-easing measures.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: SEBI allows common contact details for multiple investor accounts of HUFs, corporates, trusts
data.description:
Until now, the same contact details could only be shared between multiple accounts of family members.

The same email ID and mobile number may be given for more than one investor client if the contact details are of the authorised person of a Hindu Undivided Family, corporate, partnership or a trust, said SEBI on Tuesday.

These contact details are used by stock exchanges to send information to investors through SMS-es and e-email alerts.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

In a circular issued on December 3, the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) said that the sharing of contact details was permitted if the clients belonged to the same family. Family for this purpose would mean self, spouse, dependent children and dependent parents.

Now, after receiving representation from brokers' industry standard forum, SEBI has allowed that the contact details of an authorised person be allowed to be used for non-individual clients.

The circular listed family and authorised person for this purpose:
1.In case of individuals, self, spouse, dependent children and dependent parents.

2.In case of HUF, Karta or any of the Co-parceners as per prior approval of Karta.

3.In case of Partnership firm, any of the partners as per prior approval of all / authorised partners.

4.In case of a Trust, any of the trustees or beneficiaries as per resolution passed by the Trust.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

5.In case of Corporates, the Authorised person operating the trading account as per the Board Resolution passed by the Corporate.

The regulator said that the directions in the circular comes into immediate effect.

Stock exchanges have been asked to bring the provisions of the circular to the notice of their members and also disseminate the same on their websites. They have also been asked to make the necessary amendments to the relevant bye-laws, rules and regulations for the implementation of this direction.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Property Share Investment Trust IPO sees muted response on day 2, receives 8% subscription
data.description:
Property Share Investment Trust IPO

The initial share sale of Property Share Investment Trust, a Small and Medium REIT, saw muted response from investors on the second day of bidding (December 3) as well.

Investors have applied for 267 units of REIT in the IPO on Tuesday, which is 8 percent of the offer size of 3,361 units, the subscription data on BSE showed.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Non-institutional investors provided support to the issue, buying 32 percent units of their allotted quota, while institutional investors have not participated in the offer yet.

Up to 75 percent of net offer size has been reserved for institutional investors and the remainder 25 percent portion for non-institutional investors.

Property Share Investment Trust, the India’s first registered small and medium real estate investment trust, targets to raise Rs 353 crore through its maiden public issue during December 2-4. The price band for the offer has been fixed at Rs 10 lakh to 10.5 lakh per unit.

Click Here To Read All IPO News

The object of the REIT is to carry on the activity of a small and medium real estate investment trust through one or more schemes. The 'PropShare Platina' is the first scheme of the REIT and includes commercial office spaces in Bangalore.

The REIT, managed by investment manager PropShare Investment Manager, aims to make investment or re-investments in completed and revenue generating real estate properties under schemes through special purpose vehicles (SPVs) and other investments (including liquid assets and any business of operation and maintenance of any REIT assets).

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Remove Ad

data.title: SEBI proposes that stock exchanges do online monitoring of system audit of stock brokers
data.description:
There is already a framework for the system audit of stock brokers, which has been in place since November 13.

The market regulator has proposed a framework to enable stock exchanges to do online monitoring and supervision of the periodic system audit of stock brokers.

In a consultation paper issued on December 3, Tuesday, the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) said that this framework is being proposed to enhance the quality of the audit; to improve accountability of the auditor; to ensure that the auditor is physically visiting the brokers' premises to check the IT systems and is not outsourcing this part of the audit activity; and to help audits to submit all their evidences and reports through a web portal for speedy processing.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Also read: After BSE, SEBI stays listing of Trafiksol, which faced investor complaints about use of funds

There is already a framework for the system audit of stock brokers, which has been in place since November 13. But this system has many gaps and faults, such as allowing for audits to be done without physical inspection, allowing for poor quality of audits with inadequate samples, allowing for audits to be conducted by non-qualified people, and the nonexistence of an independent source for verification and monitoring of the pre-audit, audit and post-audit processes.

Therefore an online monitoring and supervision mechanism of the system audit has been proposed.

(This copy will be updated.)
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Swiggy to invest Rs 1,600 crore in Scootsy via rights issue, funds for Instamart's expansion and working capital
data.description:
Scootsy is in the business of supply chain services and distribution, and offers services such as warehouse management, inwarehouse processing and order fulfillment along with shipping processes for wholesalers and retailers.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

E-commerce supply chain company Scootsy will get an additional funding of up to Rs 1,600 crore in tranches from parent Swiggy through a rights issue, the company informed stock exchanges along with its quarterly results on December 3.

Swiggy said the subsidiary will get Rs 1,350 crore meant for expansion-related expenditure in the brand Instamart, while Rs 250 crore will be provided as working capital. This investment was specified by Swiggy in its IPO prospectus, and part of the corpus will be used for expansion of Scootsy's dark store network and for making lease / license payments for dark stores.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Swiggy is subscribing to the rights issue of Scootsy at a price of Rs 7,640 per share, the company said, and the investment will not lead to any change in the shareholding pattern.

Scootsy is in the business of supply chain services and distribution, and offers services such as warehouse management, in-warehouse processing and order fulfilment along with shipping processes for wholesalers and retailers.

For FY24, Scootsy clocked a turnover of Rs 5,795.7 crore, steadily growing over past three years from Rs 1,580.3 crore in FY22. Supply Chain and Distribution contributes nearly 40% to Swiggy's revenue, second only to food delivery business. For FY24, Scootsy had incurred a loss of Rs 423.97 crore.

Read more on Swiggy's first quarterly results after listing here.

Quick commerce player Swiggy also informed of plans to set up a subsidiary to enter in the business of ownership and management of sports teams, organising events, as well as acquiring broadcasting and sponsorship rights, among other key businesses.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: After BSE, SEBI stays listing of Trafiksol, which faced investor complaints about use of funds
data.description:
In September, BSE had asked the company to defer the listing since certain queries still needed answering.

After BSE, the market regulator has said that IPO of Trafiksol ITS Technologies should be put on hold pending further investigations.

In an order issued on Tuesday, the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) said that it will investigate disclosures made by the company, whose listing had been deferred following investor complaints.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Trafiksol had said that the IPO proceeds would be used partly to purchase software from third-party vendor. But according to the complaints, this third-party vendor had not filed its financial statements for more than three years.

In September, BSE had asked the company to defer the listing since certain queries still needed answering.

Also read: MC Explains: New SEBI rules on trade in index-derivatives and how they are playing out

The SEBI order, dated December 3, said that the listing of the company will continue to be on hold and that BSE should ensure that the proceeds from the IPO are placed in an interest-bearing escrow account, and no access to these funds shall be granted to Trafiksol ITS Technologies Ltd. or its affiliates until further orders.

In the order SEBI's Whole-time Member Ashwani Bhatia said that given the facts "it cannot be ruled out at this stage that the attempt to award the software contract to a Vendor, who prima facie appears to be a shell entity without any prior experience in developing a software platform of the nature disclosed by the Company in its DRHP (Draft Red Herring Prospectus), was an attempt to deliberately mislead investors and divert the IPO proceeds."

He added, "Therefore, in order to safeguard investor interest, I am of the considered view that issues raised in this Order require a detailed investigation."

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

(This copy will be updated.)
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Technical View: Nifty climbs above all key moving averages to move towards 24,800; Bank Nifty sees breakout after 2 months
data.description:
Nifty Technical View

With the Nifty 50 climbing above the 50-day and 100-day Exponential Moving Averages (EMAs), the index surpassed all key moving averages and closed decisively above the crucial hurdle of 24,350 on December 3. This marked the continuation of an upward rally for the third consecutive session in the December series. The index also saw a breakout from a downward-sloping trendline, along with a breakout from an Inverted Head and Shoulders pattern, signaling a bullish bias. The next target to watch is 24,800, which coincides with the 50 percent Fibonacci retracement from the record high to the November low, followed by the 25,000-25,200 zone. However, support is placed around 24,300, according to experts.

The Nifty 50 opened 90 points higher at 24,368, directly above the critical hurdle of 24,350, and remained in positive territory for the entire session. The index extended its upward movement as the day progressed, hitting a high of 24,481 before closing at 24,457 with above-average volumes. It gained 182 points on the day, taking the total three-day gains to nearly 400 points from last Thursday's low.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The index formed a bullish candlestick pattern with a minor lower shadow on the daily charts, finding support at a falling trendline. Meanwhile, the momentum indicator, the Relative Strength Index (RSI), stood at 55.7, indicating a positive bias.

"Technically, the pattern is indicating that the Nifty is now at the edge of breaking above the crucial hurdle of around 24,350-24,500 levels, which were the previous swing highs," said Nagaraj Shetti, Senior Technical Research Analyst at HDFC Securities.

According to Shetti, the present upside breakout could signal the negation of the previous couple of months' bearish pattern, such as lower tops and bottoms, as seen on the daily timeframe chart. Therefore, the recent swing low of 23,873 (on November 28) is expected to be a new higher bottom.

This market action suggests that the bullish chart pattern, characterized by higher tops and bottoms, is likely to unfold from here, potentially resulting in more upside for the market ahead.

Shetti believes that a decisive move above the 24,400-24,500 levels could open up the next upside target of around 24,900-25,000 in the near term. The immediate support is at 24,300, as per the concept of change in polarity.

The options data also indicates that the 24,800-25,000 range is likely to serve as strong resistance for the Nifty 50 in the short term, with support at 24,000.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

According to the weekly options data, the 25,000 strike still holds the maximum Call open interest, followed by the 24,800 and 24,500 strikes. Maximum Call writing is observed at the 24,900 strike, followed by the 24,700 and 24,850 strikes. On the Put side, maximum open interest is seen at the 23,500 strike, followed by the 24,000 and 23,800 strikes, with maximum writing at the 23,500 strike, followed by the 24,400 and 23,800 strikes.

Bank Nifty

The Bank Nifty also performed strongly and outperformed the benchmark Nifty 50, rising 587 points or 1.13 percent to 52,696. It witnessed a decisive breakout after a two-month consolidation. The index formed a bullish candlestick pattern on the daily charts after a positive opening, surpassing 52,600, and trading slightly above the 61.8 percent Fibonacci retracement (from the record high of September to the November low).

Broad-based buying was seen in the banking sector, with both PSU and private banks participating. The outperformance is expected to continue, driven by a range breakout in the Bank Nifty, which crossed 52,500 for the first time in two months, said Rajesh Bhosale, Technical Analyst at Angel One.

According to Bhosale, the bullish gap near 52,200 will serve as immediate support, while the next key resistance levels are at 53,000–53,300.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Purple United Sales set to launch its Rs 33-crore IPO on December 11
data.description:
Purple United Sales IPO

Noida-based fashion brand Purple United Sales has decided to hit Dalal Street with the aim to raise Rs 32.81 crore via initial share sale. The price band for the offer has been fixed at Rs 121-126 per share.

The IPO consists of solely fresh issue of 26.04 lakh shares by the company, with no offer-for-sale component. The public issue will open on December 11 and close on December 13, while the anchor book for institutional investors if any will be launched for a day on December 10.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Founded in 2014 by Jatinder Dev Seth, Purple United Sales caters to kids wear segment through its distribution network of 24 exclusive brand outlets (EBOs) under the brand name 'Purple United Kids' across 17 cities in India and 14 shop-in-shops in key accounts and 24 distributors across Pan India. It also operates business through major e-commerce platforms such as Myntra, Amazon, Flipkart, FirstCry, Nykaa, Hopscotch, AJJIO and more.

The company will utilise Rs 18 crore out of net fresh issue proceeds for working capital requirements, Rs 5.35 crore for opening new stores, and the remainder for general corporate purposes.

Click Here To Read All IPO News

On the financials front, it has recorded net profit at Rs 4.8 crore for the fiscal 2024, increasing sharply from Rs 1.49 crore in the previous fiscal. Revenue during the year ended March 2024 also rose significantly to Rs 42.77 crore, up from Rs 25.7 crore in the previous year. Profit for six months period ended September 2024 stood at Rs 3.3 crore on revenue of Rs 30.5 crore.

Half of the net public issue size has been reserved for qualified institutional buyers, 15 percent for non-institutional investors, and the remainder 35 percent for retail investors.

Also read: Jungle Camps India SME IPO to open on Dec 10, fixes price band at Rs 68-72 per share

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The trading in Purple United Sales shares will commence on the NSE Emerge effective December 18. Expert Global Consultants is acting as the merchant banker to the issue, while Kfin Technologies is the registrar to the offer.
data.author: MoneyControl
